items.text: Comparison operators (eq, ne, gt, lt, gte, lte) defined as predicate types in routing::predicate.
Boolean combinators (AND, OR, NOT) defined for composing predicates into arbitrarily nested expressions.
Range check predicates (field BETWEEN lower AND upper) supported for numeric and string fields.
Field-existence predicate (HAS field) defined for optional message fields.
Predicates against missing message fields evaluate to false without error.
Predicate evaluation matches against borrowed message fields without per-message heap allocation.
Short-circuit evaluation: AND stops at first false, OR stops at first true.
Predicates compile to routing functions via routing::compiler.
Compiled predicate functions produce identical routing decisions to direct predicate evaluation for all input combinations.
The compiler applies index-aware optimizations: field extraction ordering and short-circuit layout.
Routing functions are Gleam modules loaded into beamr via content-hash module loading.
Each routing function executes in a supervised beamr process with fault isolation.
A routing function receives the message and a consumer state view containing capacity and affinity tags.
Routing function crash does not affect routing evaluations for other channels.
Routing functions are hot-deployable: a new version replaces the old without dropping connections or pausing dispatch.
The routing table maps channels to active subscriptions (predicate or function).
Subscription registration and removal do not block concurrent routing evaluations.
Publishing to a channel with no matching subscriptions completes without error.
Three signal types defined in pressure::signal: accept, defer, reject.
Accept signal indicates message delivered and processing begun by consumer.
Defer signal indicates message buffered; delivery will occur when consumer capacity frees.
Reject signal indicates consumer overwhelmed; message shed per policy.
Backpressure signals are delivered to producers within the publish round-trip response, not asynchronously.
Consumers declare max in-flight messages at subscription time.
Consumers declare max buffer depth at subscription time.
Pressure monitor tracks current in-flight count and buffer depth per consumer.
Messages to a consumer at max in-flight receive defer or reject per the channel's pressure policy.
Four policy actions defined: slow-producer, shed-load, scale-consumer, alert.
Policies are configured per-channel with escalation thresholds.
Policies compose: escalating pressure triggers actions in configured sequence.
Policy enforcement evaluates automatically against monitor state without application code.
Dispatch conversation selects a consumer by evaluating the group's routing function.
Dispatch conversation links to the selected consumer process via beamr process link.
Consumer crash detected via process link triggers re-routing to another consumer within one millisecond.
Consumer groups are modeled as a routing function over a mutable consumer set.
Adding a consumer to a group takes effect on the next dispatch without pausing other consumers.
Removing a consumer from a group takes effect on the next dispatch without triggering a rebalance.
